programme.title: SOUNDS OF TRACE
programme.desc: The music never stops on TRACE Urban ! Sounds of Trace is here to make you enjoy the hottest global hits

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.sub-title: S01 : E06 Unplanned Trip Mechuka, India
programme.desc: Roshni goes to the adventure hub of Arunachal Pradesh – Mechuka. Getting a bird’s eye view of the mountains and ziplining across breathtaking vistas gives her an intense adrenaline rush that instantly makes her besotted with the town.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.sub-title: S01 : E06 10 Days Scotland Isle Of Skye
programme.desc: The grand finale of Robbie’s wild homecoming unfolds amidst the rugged beauty of the Isle of Skye.

programme.title: Xplore Oman
programme.desc: From the limestone formations of Wadi Bani Khaled to the ocean of dunes at Wahiba Sands, Elisha gets a taste of Oman’s natural heritage.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.sub-title: S01 : E07 World Spas Shangri La, Malaysia
programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries some of the signature spa treatments of the Shangri La Resort at Kota Kinabalu in Sabah, Malaysia.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: While cooking the local fish of the Jaintia region of Meghalaya, Alex also gets acquainted with the culinary heritage of Sylhet in Bangladesh.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.sub-title: S01 : E04 Great World Hotels One & Only Reethi Rah 2
programme.desc: Located in the North Malé Atoll of Maldives, the Reethi Rah resort is the perfect combination of island life mixed with world-class luxury.

programme.title: Unwind
programme.sub-title: S01 : E05 Unwind Earl's Regency, Sri Lanka
programme.desc: Indeya unwinds at Earl’s Regency, exploring its spiritual and cultural aspects. She visits a wildlife sanctuary, tries traditional meal and ending with exploring the surroundings of Kandy lake.

programme.title: World Heritage
programme.sub-title: S01 : E08 World Heritage Portugal, Portugal
programme.desc: From the impact of maritime trade and the European Renaissance on Portugal in the 15th century to the nationwide Romanticism movement in the 19th century, the heritage sites of Portugal such as Jeronimo Monastery and Pena Palace reveal various stages of the country’s evolution.

programme.title: Xplore Oman
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 Xplore Oman Wadi Bin Khalid
programme.desc: Apart from deserts, Oman also has majestic, rust-colored mountain ranges, which quietly hold the country’s history in them.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: In Kolkata, Alex meets Rajiv Neogi and Siddhartha Bose, food experts who introduce her to some of the forgotten dishes of Bengali cuisine.

programme.title: Bliss
programme.desc: With its submerged caldera, black sand beaches and purple sunsets, Santorini is the archetypal Greek island fantasy.

programme.title: Best From The Rest
programme.sub-title: S01 : E15 Best From The Rest Hong Kong Top Sees
programme.desc: A playground of steel and glass skyscrapers iced in a rainbow of neon lights and silhouetted against towering green mountain peaks, Hong Kong sets the stage for envy-inducing social media moments. Beyond the glamour, however, lies the authentic richness that makes it truly unforgettable. Here is a guide to some of the most iconic natural and cultural attractions that could help you to get up, close and personal with the beating heart of Hong Kong.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Unplanned Trip Jairampur, India
programme.desc: Roshni’s journey gets off to a rocky start, when she encounters a flat tyre on her way to Jairampur. To add to her disappointment, she realizes that she is in the town on the one day when everything around is shut. When she sets out to explore the attractions in the vicinity, however, she chances upon a World War II cemetery, which narrates riveting stories about the famous Stilwell Road. Suddenly, her day picks up.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s journey to reconnect with his Scottish roots begins in Edinburgh, the stunning capital city perched on extinct volcanoes. Navigating the twists of the Royal Mile, strutting around the majestic Edinburgh castle and learning to sip scotch like a true Scotsman, he feels a tad closer to his heritage. The real fun kicks in, however, when he stumbles onto a rather flamboyant way to unleash his inner Highlander.

programme.title: Xplore Austria
programme.desc: An opera house, which once resounded with the tunes of musical legends, a palace, which was a gift to an empress from her father: the magnificent relics of Vienna’s imperial past have many stories hidden in their walls.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Cutting-edge architecture, world-class restaurants, high-end retail, fantastical theme parks, electric nightlife – Singapore is a playground for hedonists and jetsetters. With just 48 hours on hand, London-based adventurer, Natalie Gayle has set out to explore the true character of the city. As she masters the art of the Singapore Sling, finds hidden mangrove kayaking spots and tucks into Michelin star street food, the Lion City reveals its many sides to her in the span of a weekend.

programme.title: Quest
programme.sub-title: S01 : E08 Quest Lucknow & Rewa, India
programme.desc: From a traditional Awadhi korma to Rewa’s famous Mutton Bandhwesh, Alex explores the vast and complex culinary culture of India’s royal kitchens.

programme.title: Viajar Peru
programme.desc: In the rugged heart of the Peruvian Andes, Milagros sets out on an exhilarating adventure in Huascaran National Park.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.sub-title: S01 : E02 Unplanned Trip Namsai, India
programme.desc: Roshni visits a land of lush green paddy fields and gentle rivers, of traditional bamboo houses and Buddhist temples – Namsai in Arunachal Pradesh. The highlight of this idyllic town, the Golden Pagoda, leaves her spellbound. Here, she spends a day with the monks and finds out the secret behind the peaceful lives they lead.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s adventures in Perth kick off with him immersing himself in the historic grandeur of the Scone Castle, where a mysterious stone tells tales of Scottish monarchs. But the real thunder of the Highlands awaits at the Braemar Games gathering, where tartan meets brawn, and Robbie learns that embracing his Scottish side involves more than just kilts and castles.

programme.title: Xplore Austria
programme.desc: The magnificently preserved palaces and monuments of Vienna make it a gorgeous place to pretend you’re in the past. When Elisha visits the city, however, she discovers its contemporary side. She soaks in the energy of the city at its action-packed corners like a hotspot for modern art and cultural events, a bustling market and a Swarovski showroom.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.sub-title: S01 : E25 City Breaks Hyderabad, India
programme.desc: In a 48-hour escape from boardrooms and deadlines, Sukruti finds herself in the enchanting city of pearls: Hyderabad.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: Be it recipes that evolved from hunting or dishes that were prepared as offerings to god, Alex uncovers the secrets of royal Bagheli cuisine.

programme.title: Viajar Peru
programme.desc: Dramatic views of the Pacific Ocean, brightly colored colonial buildings, world-class cuisine – the capital city of Peru, Lima has a lot going for it. Milagros reveals the treasures of her home city from the diverse neighborhoods, like the upscale Miraflores to the bohemian Barranco. She also shows a side of Lima where one can enjoy fun adventures with the marine creatures.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 Unplanned Trip Hawai, India
programme.desc: On a visit to Hawai, the land of the Mishmi tribe, Roshni meets a tribe member who teaches her how to catch fish. Later that day, she stays at the house of a Mishmi family, and they treat her to a local drink that makes her shed her inhibitions and open up to them in no time.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: A modern European oil city built on the back of a rich maritime heritage, Aberdeen is the energetic kingpin of northeast Scotland. While the boom times may come and go, the dynamic coastal metropolis never ceases to thrill with its free museums, outdoor adventures and natural attractions right on the city’s doorstep.

programme.title: Xplore Austria
programme.desc: Elisha is on a trip to Salzburg, the birthplace of Mozart, whose legacy still echoes throughout Salzburg’s Baroque residences, auditoriums and dining halls. Paying her respects at the renowned composer’s memorial, and attending a musical based on the story of the Von Trapp family, she finds herself starstruck by the rich musical heritage of this charming city.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.sub-title: S01 : E27 City Breaks Bengaluru, India
programme.desc: When Yogita’s business plans in Bengaluru unravel, she ends up with 48 hours of leisure and no idea what to do in the city. Enter Shruti – her friend, and a diehard fan of the city she calls home. From quiet mornings in one of Bengaluru’s many parks to lively evenings at one of its many breweries, from rustic Kannada feasts to thrilling adventures – Shruti wants Yogita to experience it all. But are two days enough for her to make Yogita fall in love with the city that has captured her heart?

programme.title: World Spas
programme.sub-title: S01 : E07 World Spas Shangri La, Malaysia
programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries some of the signature spa treatments of the Shangri La Resort at Kota Kinabalu in Sabah, Malaysia.

programme.title: Viajar Peru
programme.desc: In the last leg of her journey, Milagros goes to Trujillo, where she introduces the flavors of the northern region of Peru and also Marinera – the sensuous national dance. But the highlight in Trujillo are the forgotten sites like Chan Chan and Huaca del Moche, where secrets from Peru’s pre-Incan past begin to unfold.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.sub-title: S01 : E04 Unplanned Trip Roing, India
programme.desc: Crisp mountain breezes, lush greenery and gurgling streams – Roing is a town that entices Roshni to explore it on foot. A visit to the local market leads to her sampling an infamous chilli that has reduced seasoned foodies to tears. Later on, she takes a tour of the “Orange Bowl of Arunachal Pradesh”.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Since medieval times Aberdeenshire and its northwestern neighbour Moray have been the richest and most fertile regions of the Highlands. Aberdeenshire is famed for its Aberdeen Angus beef cattle, its many fine castles and the prosperous 'granite city' of Aberdeen.

programme.title: Xplore Austria
programme.desc: On a trip to Innsbruck, Elisha is instantly charmed by the Old Town, where she feasts her eyes on the city’s iconic attractions. From the famous ‘Golden Roof’ to the Imperial Church, the monuments she visits reveal to her stories of a 15th century emperor, under whose reign the city had reached its zenith.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.sub-title: S01 : E13 World Spas Maya Ubud, Indonesia
programme.desc: At Maya Ubud Spa Resort, Jamie Srubis tries different spa treatments that induce the same tranquility which Bali does.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: While learning to prepare a Naga pork delicacy, Alex gets an understanding of the different dishes and cooking styles of Nagaland’s tribes.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.sub-title: S01 : E02 Great World Hotels Closenberg, Sri Lanka
programme.desc: Moeen, a travel enthusiast, goes for a short getaway to the Closenberg Hotel in Galle, an 18th century Dutch walled town in Sri Lanka with grand colonial houses and beautiful beaches. At the resort, he chances upon a traditional Sri Lankan wedding, which offers him a glimpse of the country’s traditions and cuisine.

programme.title: Quest
programme.sub-title: S01 : E12 Quest Nagaland & Shillong, India
programme.desc: From traditional smoked pork in Nagaland to frog meat in Shillong, Alex finds out the secrets of tribal cuisine in Nagaland.

programme.title: Xplore Oman
programme.desc: From the magnificent Sultan Qaboos Grand Mosque to the enchanting Muttrah Souq, Elisha experiences the classic Arabic charms of Oman, in Muscat.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.sub-title: S01 : E05 Unplanned Trip Pasighat, India
programme.desc: Did you know one of the world’s most expensive varieties of tea is produced in Arunachal Pradesh? Roshni attends a tea festival at the Donyi Polo Tea Estate in Pasighat, where she gets a chance to savour some of the most premium varieties of tea produced in the state.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 Great World Hotels One & Only Reethi Rah 1
programme.desc: From swimming in the ocean to relishing a traditional Maldivian lobster curry, Kim experiences the true meaning of joie de vivre at Reethi Rah.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.sub-title: S01 : E09 World Spas Mines Wellness, Thailand
programme.desc: Shirodhara is a traditional Ayurvedic oil drip treatment that popular world traveler, Jamie Srubis tries at the Mines Wellness Resort in Malaysia. Following this, she tries other treatments such as Urut Melayu, a full body massage which derives its technique from a traditional Malay martial art, and Acupuncture and Moxibustion, the two oldest treatments of Traditional Chinese Medicine.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: In the enchanting landscapes of Inverness, Robbie’s journey takes a whimsical turn: from eccentric to downright magical.

programme.title: Quest
programme.sub-title: S01 : E12 Quest Nagaland & Shillong, India
programme.desc: From traditional smoked pork in Nagaland to frog meat in Shillong, Alex finds out the secrets of tribal cuisine in Nagaland.

programme.title: Xplore Oman
programme.desc: From the magnificent Sultan Qaboos Grand Mosque to the enchanting Muttrah Souq, Elisha experiences the classic Arabic charms of Oman, in Muscat.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.sub-title: S01 : E05 Unplanned Trip Pasighat, India
programme.desc: Did you know one of the world’s most expensive varieties of tea is produced in Arunachal Pradesh? Roshni attends a tea festival at the Donyi Polo Tea Estate in Pasighat, where she gets a chance to savour some of the most premium varieties of tea produced in the state.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 Great World Hotels One & Only Reethi Rah 1
programme.desc: From swimming in the ocean to relishing a traditional Maldivian lobster curry, Kim experiences the true meaning of joie de vivre at Reethi Rah.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.sub-title: S01 : E09 World Spas Mines Wellness, Thailand
programme.desc: Shirodhara is a traditional Ayurvedic oil drip treatment that popular world traveler, Jamie Srubis tries at the Mines Wellness Resort in Malaysia. Following this, she tries other treatments such as Urut Melayu, a full body massage which derives its technique from a traditional Malay martial art, and Acupuncture and Moxibustion, the two oldest treatments of Traditional Chinese Medicine.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: In the enchanting landscapes of Inverness, Robbie’s journey takes a whimsical turn: from eccentric to downright magical.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: In Kolkata, Alex meets Rajiv Neogi and Siddhartha Bose, food experts who introduce her to some of the forgotten dishes of Bengali cuisine.

programme.title: Unwind
programme.sub-title: S01 : E05 Unwind Earl's Regency, Sri Lanka
programme.desc: Indeya unwinds at Earl’s Regency, exploring its spiritual and cultural aspects. She visits a wildlife sanctuary, tries traditional meal and ending with exploring the surroundings of Kandy lake.

programme.title: Bliss
programme.desc: With its submerged caldera, black sand beaches and purple sunsets, Santorini is the archetypal Greek island fantasy.

programme.title: Best From The Rest
programme.sub-title: S01 : E15 Best From The Rest Hong Kong Top Sees
programme.desc: A playground of steel and glass skyscrapers iced in a rainbow of neon lights and silhouetted against towering green mountain peaks, Hong Kong sets the stage for envy-inducing social media moments. Beyond the glamour, however, lies the authentic richness that makes it truly unforgettable. Here is a guide to some of the most iconic natural and cultural attractions that could help you to get up, close and personal with the beating heart of Hong Kong.

programme.title: World Heritage
programme.sub-title: S01 : E08 World Heritage Portugal, Portugal
programme.desc: From the impact of maritime trade and the European Renaissance on Portugal in the 15th century to the nationwide Romanticism movement in the 19th century, the heritage sites of Portugal such as Jeronimo Monastery and Pena Palace reveal various stages of the country’s evolution.

programme.title: Xplore Oman
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 Xplore Oman Wadi Bin Khalid
programme.desc: Apart from deserts, Oman also has majestic, rust-colored mountain ranges, which quietly hold the country’s history in them.

programme.title: Quest
programme.sub-title: S01 : E08 Quest Lucknow & Rewa, India
programme.desc: From a traditional Awadhi korma to Rewa’s famous Mutton Bandhwesh, Alex explores the vast and complex culinary culture of India’s royal kitchens.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Unplanned Trip Jairampur, India
programme.desc: Roshni’s journey gets off to a rocky start, when she encounters a flat tyre on her way to Jairampur. To add to her disappointment, she realizes that she is in the town on the one day when everything around is shut. When she sets out to explore the attractions in the vicinity, however, she chances upon a World War II cemetery, which narrates riveting stories about the famous Stilwell Road. Suddenly, her day picks up.

programme.title: Viajar Peru
programme.desc: In the rugged heart of the Peruvian Andes, Milagros sets out on an exhilarating adventure in Huascaran National Park.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s journey to reconnect with his Scottish roots begins in Edinburgh, the stunning capital city perched on extinct volcanoes. Navigating the twists of the Royal Mile, strutting around the majestic Edinburgh castle and learning to sip scotch like a true Scotsman, he feels a tad closer to his heritage. The real fun kicks in, however, when he stumbles onto a rather flamboyant way to unleash his inner Highlander.

programme.title: Xplore Austria
programme.desc: An opera house, which once resounded with the tunes of musical legends, a palace, which was a gift to an empress from her father: the magnificent relics of Vienna’s imperial past have many stories hidden in their walls.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Cutting-edge architecture, world-class restaurants, high-end retail, fantastical theme parks, electric nightlife – Singapore is a playground for hedonists and jetsetters. With just 48 hours on hand, London-based adventurer, Natalie Gayle has set out to explore the true character of the city. As she masters the art of the Singapore Sling, finds hidden mangrove kayaking spots and tucks into Michelin star street food, the Lion City reveals its many sides to her in the span of a weekend.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: Be it recipes that evolved from hunting or dishes that were prepared as offerings to god, Alex uncovers the secrets of royal Bagheli cuisine.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.sub-title: S01 : E02 Unplanned Trip Namsai, India
programme.desc: Roshni visits a land of lush green paddy fields and gentle rivers, of traditional bamboo houses and Buddhist temples – Namsai in Arunachal Pradesh. The highlight of this idyllic town, the Golden Pagoda, leaves her spellbound. Here, she spends a day with the monks and finds out the secret behind the peaceful lives they lead.

programme.title: Viajar Peru
programme.desc: Dramatic views of the Pacific Ocean, brightly colored colonial buildings, world-class cuisine – the capital city of Peru, Lima has a lot going for it. Milagros reveals the treasures of her home city from the diverse neighborhoods, like the upscale Miraflores to the bohemian Barranco. She also shows a side of Lima where one can enjoy fun adventures with the marine creatures.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s adventures in Perth kick off with him immersing himself in the historic grandeur of the Scone Castle, where a mysterious stone tells tales of Scottish monarchs. But the real thunder of the Highlands awaits at the Braemar Games gathering, where tartan meets brawn, and Robbie learns that embracing his Scottish side involves more than just kilts and castles.

programme.title: Xplore Austria
programme.desc: The magnificently preserved palaces and monuments of Vienna make it a gorgeous place to pretend you’re in the past. When Elisha visits the city, however, she discovers its contemporary side. She soaks in the energy of the city at its action-packed corners like a hotspot for modern art and cultural events, a bustling market and a Swarovski showroom.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.sub-title: S01 : E25 City Breaks Hyderabad, India
programme.desc: In a 48-hour escape from boardrooms and deadlines, Sukruti finds herself in the enchanting city of pearls: Hyderabad.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.sub-title: S01 : E07 World Spas Shangri La, Malaysia
programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries some of the signature spa treatments of the Shangri La Resort at Kota Kinabalu in Sabah, Malaysia.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 Unplanned Trip Hawai, India
programme.desc: On a visit to Hawai, the land of the Mishmi tribe, Roshni meets a tribe member who teaches her how to catch fish. Later that day, she stays at the house of a Mishmi family, and they treat her to a local drink that makes her shed her inhibitions and open up to them in no time.

programme.title: Viajar Peru
programme.desc: In the last leg of her journey, Milagros goes to Trujillo, where she introduces the flavors of the northern region of Peru and also Marinera – the sensuous national dance. But the highlight in Trujillo are the forgotten sites like Chan Chan and Huaca del Moche, where secrets from Peru’s pre-Incan past begin to unfold.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: A modern European oil city built on the back of a rich maritime heritage, Aberdeen is the energetic kingpin of northeast Scotland. While the boom times may come and go, the dynamic coastal metropolis never ceases to thrill with its free museums, outdoor adventures and natural attractions right on the city’s doorstep.

programme.title: Xplore Austria
programme.desc: Elisha is on a trip to Salzburg, the birthplace of Mozart, whose legacy still echoes throughout Salzburg’s Baroque residences, auditoriums and dining halls. Paying her respects at the renowned composer’s memorial, and attending a musical based on the story of the Von Trapp family, she finds herself starstruck by the rich musical heritage of this charming city.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.sub-title: S01 : E27 City Breaks Bengaluru, India
programme.desc: When Yogita’s business plans in Bengaluru unravel, she ends up with 48 hours of leisure and no idea what to do in the city. Enter Shruti – her friend, and a diehard fan of the city she calls home. From quiet mornings in one of Bengaluru’s many parks to lively evenings at one of its many breweries, from rustic Kannada feasts to thrilling adventures – Shruti wants Yogita to experience it all. But are two days enough for her to make Yogita fall in love with the city that has captured her heart?

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: While learning to prepare a Naga pork delicacy, Alex gets an understanding of the different dishes and cooking styles of Nagaland’s tribes.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.sub-title: S01 : E04 Unplanned Trip Roing, India
programme.desc: Crisp mountain breezes, lush greenery and gurgling streams – Roing is a town that entices Roshni to explore it on foot. A visit to the local market leads to her sampling an infamous chilli that has reduced seasoned foodies to tears. Later on, she takes a tour of the “Orange Bowl of Arunachal Pradesh”.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.sub-title: S01 : E02 Great World Hotels Closenberg, Sri Lanka
programme.desc: Moeen, a travel enthusiast, goes for a short getaway to the Closenberg Hotel in Galle, an 18th century Dutch walled town in Sri Lanka with grand colonial houses and beautiful beaches. At the resort, he chances upon a traditional Sri Lankan wedding, which offers him a glimpse of the country’s traditions and cuisine.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Since medieval times Aberdeenshire and its northwestern neighbour Moray have been the richest and most fertile regions of the Highlands. Aberdeenshire is famed for its Aberdeen Angus beef cattle, its many fine castles and the prosperous 'granite city' of Aberdeen.

programme.title: Xplore Austria
programme.desc: On a trip to Innsbruck, Elisha is instantly charmed by the Old Town, where she feasts her eyes on the city’s iconic attractions. From the famous ‘Golden Roof’ to the Imperial Church, the monuments she visits reveal to her stories of a 15th century emperor, under whose reign the city had reached its zenith.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.sub-title: S01 : E13 World Spas Maya Ubud, Indonesia
programme.desc: At Maya Ubud Spa Resort, Jamie Srubis tries different spa treatments that induce the same tranquility which Bali does.

programme.title: Quest
programme.sub-title: S01 : E12 Quest Nagaland & Shillong, India
programme.desc: From traditional smoked pork in Nagaland to frog meat in Shillong, Alex finds out the secrets of tribal cuisine in Nagaland.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.sub-title: S01 : E05 Unplanned Trip Pasighat, India
programme.desc: Did you know one of the world’s most expensive varieties of tea is produced in Arunachal Pradesh? Roshni attends a tea festival at the Donyi Polo Tea Estate in Pasighat, where she gets a chance to savour some of the most premium varieties of tea produced in the state.

programme.title: Xplore Oman
programme.desc: From the limestone formations of Wadi Bani Khaled to the ocean of dunes at Wahiba Sands, Elisha gets a taste of Oman’s natural heritage.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.sub-title: S01 : E06 Unplanned Trip Mechuka, India

programme.title: Paseka: The Easter Elephant
programme.desc: On Easter morning, a baby elephant is mysteriously abandoned by her herd in the wilds of Botswana's Okavango Delta. Saved from certain death by a bizarre chain of events, she forces herself into a very unusual, captive elephant herd. This is "Paseka's" story. We follow her first year in this spectacular African wilderness, learning daily what it means to be an elephant. What is instinctive? What is she taught by the elephants? What does she need to learn from the humans? How does her arrival change the herd dynamics? Her unique journey is our window into the elephant world.

programme.title: Otters and the Exotic Pet Trade
programme.desc: Tracking the exotic pet trade of otters across the globe, is a story of gangsters and guns, mammals, and middlemen. Japanese otter cafés and social media influencers are driving increased demand for pet otters all over the world, with devastating consequences. These otters might be adorable, but the truth behind this trade is anything but. Ripped from the wild or irresponsibly bred, orphaned by poachers and confined in unnatural environments, these otters live a life of extreme misery. Following World Animal Protection intelligence, environmental photojournalist Aaron Gekoski investigates Otters and the Exotic Pet Trade.

programme.title: People of the Ice Bridge

programme.title: Mission Blue: Harbouring Hope
programme.desc: The oceans are the source of life on Earth, yet despite their vastness they are in trouble. Mission Blue, a Perpetual Planet Initiative partner, ignites change through a global network of “Hope Spots”, marine-protected areas of critical vulnerability.

programme.title: Cocaine Hippos
programme.desc: Originally Pablo Escobar's playthings, more than one hundred African hippos are now an invasive species in Colombia. Can a global team of conservationists, lawyers, and even local residents find a way to save these unwanted guests?

programme.title: Challenge the Gap with Paul Black
programme.desc: Challenge the Gap with Paul Black is a fun yet investigative social short following the Glaswegian Tik-Tok star on a journey of discovery as he meets innovative businesses in his home city who are leading the way when it comes to shifting towards a circular economy. Helping to break down barriers on the topic of circularity, Paul will undertake a series of challenges inspired by the local businesses he'll meet. Supported by the Glasgow Chamber of Commerce, our film champions those who are challenging the traditional, linear methods of creation, design and action, and sets out why we must all join the fight to help close the circularity gap.

programme.title: Dancing on Icebergs

programme.title: Sharks: The Big Five

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Idoia (Spain)
programme.desc: Idoia thrives on weaving together the sources of inspiration that surround her. Heavily influenced by the natural beauty of her home region of Galicia, Spain, she creates unique woven pieces from diverse and beautiful materials. Idoia’s pieces represent her vision of a modern woman, drawing from the traditions and experiences of those who perfected the craft before her and also pushing to innovate and adapt her creations for the modern world. Her recent collaboration with luxury fashion house Loewe is a dream come true for this modern artisan.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The Auxerre country
programme.desc: Auxerre is cut by valleys whose best exposed slopes are home to vineyards. The meandering course of the Yonne runs vertically through this region. To the east of Auxerre, the Chablis region is rounded with hills. The vineyard, extending on both sides of the valley of the Serein. To the south, lined by the Nivernais canal, the upper Yonne valley attracts climbing enthusiasts and boaters, before being swollen by the waters of the Cure. The region is renowned throughout the world for the quality of its wines, particularly those of Chablis. Julie begins her journey in Auxerre and then proposes typical recipes from the area: croquignoles and cherry clafoutis.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The chestnut route
programme.desc: Julie takes to the road in her little red car to honour one of her favourite fruits: the chestnut. Julie will meet several producers in Ardèche, cook the traditional soup of the region with the sparkling Marithé. She will also make the recipe for homemade marrons glacés, before heading to Corsica to work with chestnut flour and the leaves of her tree. This journey will end as always with a clever chef's recipe, and this time it will be Claude Brioude who will do it! This chef likes to shake up the rules and will work the chestnut with fish, two seasonal products that we are not usually used to combining.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 1 - Everything is living and everything is linked - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Everything is living and everything is linked - Part 2
programme.desc: Through the most magnificent images and amazing human stories, Man is at the heart of the biodiversity that he must respect and preserve. We go to China in the province of Yunan, where a plant with exceptional virtues against malaria has been discovered. Then we travel to Africa, where man's intervention in the ecosystem of Lake Victoria has triggered a considerable imbalance. We will also explore to Greenland to meet the polar bears, the first victims of the melting ice.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Daniel Hautot
programme.desc: He had a real professional rise, quickly moving from commercial to sales manager. But the awareness quickly catches up with him too: what is the purpose of all this? Is it a real pursuit of happiness? Daniel keeps his acquired skills and puts them to the benefit of his passion for yoga. Follow Daniel until the opening of his yoga & community space.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Guillaume Nivelle
programme.desc: To make a profession representative of his personality is the discovery made by Guillaume at twenty-six. After several years as a bartender and crossing a dark period, Guillaume discovers nature and falls in love with it. Accompany Guillaume in his passion, in the middle of a refitted nature.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Laura Belfadla
programme.desc: Laura will not tell you the whole truth because she does not want a system where you are told what to do. After several years as head of business development in law firm, Laura changes her life and creates the plant academy. Let yourself go for a healthier life.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Laurence Sala
programme.desc: It took a burnout and several miscarriages to Laurence to take a new path. It's finally a bad thing for a good. Throughout her ordeals, Laurence will know how to listen to her needs, her desires and her passions. With a surprise, follow Laurence in her life as a mother and naturopath.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The country of Fontainebleau
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 The country of Fontainebleau
programme.desc: This week, it is 80 kms from the capital, in Fontainebleau, that Julie is putting down her bags. Fontainebleau, a name famous throughout the world, a magical name which evokes for some a forest, for others a castle, a name which still shines with its royal history. The forest of Fontainebleau has inspired the art world since the beginning of the 19th century. Landscape painters such as Corot and Millet drew inspiration for their works there. But Fontainebleau has many other treasures in its bag, and gastronomy is part of its heritage. This is what Julie will discover through three recipes: Quail with grapes, raspberry and poppy pie and crayfish grave.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Fish on the menu
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Fish on the menu
programme.desc: Julie takes us to the southern Brittany and more precisely the maritime district of Guilvinec to follow the fish route from the nets to your plate. Le Guilvinec may be the first French artisanal fishing port, but all the seashores of France have good seasonal products. Julie drives up to the south to discover several farmed fish in an aquaculture farm in the Mediterranean, while cooking sea bass in Noirmoutier, monkfish in the north and even cod in the huge market at Rungis. Julie challenged Chef Olivier Bellin, who lives in Plomodiern in Finistère, to cook a little-known fish: the wrasse.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 1 - Defending water is defending life - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Defending water is defending life - Part 1
programme.desc: He takes us to Petra in Jordan. We discover how the Nabataeans became specialists in collecting rainwater, which they managed meticulously. Then, we find ourselves in Las Vegas, on the verge of a shortage, and in Amman, Jordan, to discover the daily difficulties linked to the shortage and rationing of water. In Israel, the Dead Sea is disappearing. Due to intensive agriculture, the Jordan River and other rivers no longer supply it with enough water. In France, the Loire is for nature lovers, today a multitude of species have come to recolonise the river, including the famous wild salmon. In Jordan, in the red desert of Wadi Rum, we meet Khaleel Maiteq, who shares with us his life and that...

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Acrobatic Yoga

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Potato cutlets with vegetable purée
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Potato cutlets with vegetable purée
programme.desc: In this recipe, we'll prepare delicious potato cutlets accompanied by a creamy vegetable purée. The cutlets are crispy on the outside with Japanese breadcrumbs and tender on the inside, while the celeriac puree adds a touch of sweetness and lightness. This dish is perfect for a comforting and tasty vegetarian meal.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Myriam
programme.desc: A French-born social worker leaves her anxiety-riddled routine to go on holiday to the Philippines, and sees an opportunity to change her life in the process. The abundance of mangoes on the island of Palawan inspires her to begin her own homemade jam business, ethically sourced and using recycled jars. She now lives out her tropical dream life, away from urban-life stress.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Yesmin
programme.desc: Born in Mexico but always attracted to new cultures and countries, Yesmin worked many jobs until she finally found her calling. She decided to merge her passions for art and travel and became a “Vagabond Artist”. She is now able to travel the world while making a living by painting beautiful murals in her own distinct style.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Jessica
programme.desc: Jessica’s life as a teenager was marked by a family tragedy that she struggled to cope with for a long time. Her anxiety had reached an all-time high and she was experiencing regular panic attacks when finally, she discovered surfing. This newfound passion thoroughly changed her lifestyle, ultimately resulting in a move to Bali, Indonesia. Today, she runs mindful surf & yoga retreats while also working online as a wellness writer.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Lisa & Pete
programme.desc: Two urbanites surprise themselves by changing their city lifestyles for farm living : Lisa, an architect based in London, and her husband Pete, an actor seen in Hollywoodian movies such as “Ghost in the Shell” alongside Scarlett Johansson and a rock star in his own right in his native Malaysia. Together, they figured they were far from being ready for retirement. Instead, they dove headfirst into running an organic farm where they now both thrive while taking pleasure in returning to a simpler life.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The Land of the Impressionists
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 The Land of the Impressionists
programme.desc: It is in Giverny that Julie's journey begins, the place whose name resounds as the cradle of Impressionism has kept a moving authenticity. Its fame is strongly linked to the historical presence of Claude Monet and to all the fabulous witnesses left by him. Claude Monet also left his mark on the cooking in Giverny. His recipe books are there to testify to this. It’s with these notebooks that Julie will be inspired to fill hers, on the menu: Rabbit in compote, andouilles puff pastry with camembert, and apricot charlottes.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Game season
programme.desc: We headed to the Loir-et-Cher for a hunt supervised by the ONCFS teams. Let's go to the Vosges to cook a tasty deer jig with Mauricette. Let's meet Pierre in the Côte d'Or where he makes hunting horns, an essential tool for hunting. Julie will also meet two hunters from Gironde for a very original afternoon of hunting by the barrel before joining Joël in Aquitaine for another game bird recipe. We will then talk with Laurent about the job of dog trainer. This time it will be Frédéric Robert who will concoct a recipe for hare rubbed with cocoa and cooked in a clay sarcophagus.

programme.desc: Origami is about the art of paper folding, a Japanese and Chinese millenary tradition. In modern usage, the word “origami” is used as an inclusive term for all paper folding, regardless of their cultural origin. Different folding and sculpting techniques, new models, bright colors – you’ll see all of this in our program! We will teach you how to transform a flat square sheet of paper into a finished sculpture – from simple to complex!

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 5 - Concentration based on sensory perceptions
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Concentration based on sensory perceptions
programme.desc: Our five senses, sight, smell, hearing, touch and taste are the best means with which to achieve relaxing and soothing sensations. The senses allow us to connect with the outside world and to create a representation of things. The aim of this session is to learn to focus our attention on a certain perception in order to allow the body and mind to rest.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 6 - Self-massage for the arms
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Self-massage for the arms
programme.desc: In order to feel in charge of your body, MyZen TV offers you this guided self-massage exercise. The aim is to relax and regain your natural body tone so that you can face any future events with strength and control.

programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Coping with stress on the subway…

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 1 - Defending water is defending life - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Defending water is defending life - Part 2
programme.desc: We find ourselves in Israel in the Negev desert, Elaine is looking for solutions to combat desertification. The object of her research: trees, which consume little water. In Mali, we meet the Dogon: the desert farmers. He shows us his people's recipes for managing water. In this part of Mali, where it almost never rains. In Palestine, the village of Wadi Fukeen. A village where farmers use an irrigation and water-sharing system that is over 300 years old and based on respect and trust. And in Gaza, Palestine, where specialists are fighting to save Palestinian children made ill by unsafe water. In the Jordan Valley, where three countries in conflict face the same problems: the lack of...

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Hot Yoga Session
programme.desc: Always on the look for looking for new detox discoveries, Val leads us to Lifehouse’s tonic and elixirs. Here, she discovers the range of elixir with therapeutic virtues exhilarating and relaxing. Before leaving, we made a hook by Jack's lab to prepare our own elixir. A private hot Yoga session then awaited Val at the popular Urban Exhale Hot Yoga where wearing a swimsuit could be welcomed. This episode ends on a fruity note as we tasted smoothies with unusual names that seduced Val, especially the 72 and Sun.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Maud Urbas
programme.desc: It is a disease, managed for years by ineffective drugs, which brings Maud to consult a naturopath. This meeting will be the trigger for a revelation, a new choice of life turned to the well being of the other through coaching. Follow Maud's changes every day to turn to her well-being.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Ika
programme.desc: After graduating with a Law degree, Ika built a career as a legal manager and advisor. For a decade she worked on risk-related matters. One day, she decided to take a big calculated risk of her own and give up her law-life to pursue a passion for jewelry-making. Her creations are inspired by her travels and the natural materials surrounding her.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Hansen
programme.desc: A former model and Sports News anchor, Hansen has drifted away from the limelight to focus on a personal journey. His Yoga trip began 10 years ago, after suffering a herniated disc injury. His experiences have taught him that the only way one can truly change the world is to raise the consciousness of humanity, and this actually starts within ourselves.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Mark, Camille & Juliana
programme.desc: A Filipino couple with a shared love for traveling decide to change their lives and explore a passion for eco-tourism. Their glamping tents are perched on the hills of Bacuit Bay and promise a natural and enriching experience. Daily sunset yoga sessions and Farm-to-Table dining are just some of the activities they share with their guests. Their energy attracts like-minded people on similar journeys, notably their yoga teacher Juliana who has an inspiring story of her own to share with us.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Lille country
programme.desc: Head north to discover a French city that cannot be ignored: Lille. The city has an exceptional architectural heritage, a merchant town since the Middle Ages, a citadel under Louis XIV, an industrial town in the 19th century... it expanded over the centuries. Julie will meet those who contribute to the fame of the city and its region, through its culinary heritage with the menu of this journey: the recipe of the coq à la bière, that of the hochepot and for a sweet note, a crème brûlée with chicory.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The flavors of olive oil
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 The flavors of olive oil
programme.desc: Julie is offering you a trip to the south of France. Between the Rhone Valley and the Mediterranean, let's discover a fruit that is harvested in November: the olive. On the way, we will stop at the Sourdon family's house to cook sea bream accompanied by an olive fricot. We will then go to Aix en Provence to prepare a strange dessert: the Fernand-style oil pump. Then we head for an exceptional estate, the Villa Beaulieu, to taste a Bagna Cauda like you have rarely seen before finishing with Jean-Luc Rabanel, a starred organic chef and defenders of grand cru olive oil...

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 1 - The sea needs us as we need the sea - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 The sea needs us as we need the sea - Part 1
programme.desc: The oceans cover more than 70% of the Earth's surface. The sea is the largest natural environment on our planet and remains virtually unexploited. In Corsica, from a tower on Capo Rosso and in the Calanques de Piana. In the Galapagos, we have made a portrait of Paul Watson, ecologist and co-founder of Greenpeace. This Canadian with the physique of an adventurer has taken up the defence of marine animals. In Corsica, in the Bouches de Bonifacio, the Mediterranean basin is one of the richest in biodiversity on the planet. In Corsica, with overfishing. Worldwide, 75% of fish stocks are threatened by overfishing. The cause? Large-scale, wasteful and insufficiently regulated fishing. In...

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Turtle Conservatory Center
programme.desc: Breathtaking views, quality products and two impressive initiatives. That's what is ready for you in this episode. A first step takes us to Fabien's winery, 'the Ojai Vineyard'. This place in the heart of Ojai's nature combines the best of California production with an unexpected 'French touch' by bringing his equipment from France. We then visit 'The Turtle Conservatory Center', a reserve where rare species of turtles gather from around the world. Respectful of their needs and living conditions, this center is a haven of peace for turtles and Val, who will definitely come back here on her day off.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Zoe Boivin
programme.desc: To know how to listen, it can take a long time as it can happen in a revelation. It's a real revelation that led Zoe to listen to herself, to hear a destiny that dedicated her to an artistic career. From her studio to a vernissage, from her family to a workshop given in a school, share Zoe's adventure to the path that truly corresponds to her.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Claire
programme.desc: One woman’s personal mission to reduce her household rubbish turned into a passion that gave birth to Southeast Asia’s first Zero-Waste Bulk Food shop. Claire is a driven and successful mother of three who turned away from her corporate job to channel her business-savvy into reducing our impact on the environment and contributing to the improvement of her local community.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Zaira (Argentina)
programme.desc: For a long time Zaira let herself be defined solely as a survivor of the traumatic sexual assault that marked her life. In her healing search, she left behind the busy city life and moved to seek solace in the mountains of Mendoza. It is among these magnificent peaks that Zaira found peace within herself and harmony with her surroundings. Now, eager to help others heal, she is the leader of a trekking initiative that doubles as a support group for other women who are victims of sexual aggression.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Ile (Argentina)
programme.desc: Ileana studied business and worked long hours indoors for a multinational logistics company. Her love for dance and desire for movement drove her to quit her corporate life and begin a new chapter as a fitness trainer. Quickly exhausting the traditional methods available at her local gyms, she began to create innovative and groundbreaking exercise methods of her own. Her goal: to empower women by making them feel strong and feminine at the same time.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The Country of Le Mans
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 The Country of Le Mans
programme.desc: It is in the heart of the Sarthe that Julie begins her new tour. Located in the Pays de la Loire region, the Sarthe department is the gateway to the French Grand Ouest. In the Haut Maine region, Le Mans is a city of art as well as a historic city, which played a decisive role in the development of the automobile. The country of Le Mans is distinguished by the diversity of its heritage, having kept in its stones the imprints of all eras. This region is notably renowned for its poulet de Loué, its capon and many other regional specialities. Let's go for a real Sarthe gourmet journey, with veal rouelles à la mancelle, roast poularde and seasonal fruit tart.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Seashells on the menu
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Seashells on the menu
programme.desc: We will start this journey near La Rochelle to learn a little more about the breeding of the famous bouchot mussels but also a very particular technique to cook them. We will then go on a tour of France's best oysters: towards Cancale, Arcachon, Montpellier and finally Oléron to learn about the different breeding methods and to find out more about all the species in France. Julie will cook a fricassee of berniques, the little Chinese hats that cover the rocks of the coast, as well as pink clams that are little known to the public. We will continue our journey in Finistère to meet Sylvain, the only abalone breeder in the country, and then to Méru where there are still workshops magnifying...

programme.desc: The oceans cover more than 70% of the Earth's surface. The sea is the largest natural environment on our planet and remains virtually unexploited. In Brittany, in the Seven Islands. Then in the Bay of Saint-Brieuc, in the middle of a sea of green algae. In Brittany, intensive agriculture has developed over the last thirty years. In New Caledonia, the coral reef is the second largest in the world and could be threatened by the construction of a nickel mine. In Normandy, in the Botanical Garden of Vauville, an extraordinary garden has plants from all over the world. Also in the Archipelago of Chausey in Normandy with the highest tides in Europe. Or the Mont Saint Michel. As a result of...

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Yoga with Goats

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - David Jeannerot
programme.desc: David Jeannerot left the world of fashion and abandoned his career as artistic director to become a plant. An exceptional florist who wishes to give back all its place to nature in the city.David had a dream life. He traveled around the world for major ready-to-wear brands. But under the appearance David was actually very isolated. To give meaning to his life, and to go in the direction of his personal convictions, he decides to become a plantist. He arranges corners of greenery on the smallest window sill with the aim of bringing nature to the city, and to make Paris greener.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Agostina (Argentina)
programme.desc: When Agostina’s mother was diagnosed with breast cancer, she began seeking out soft natural fabrics that would not irritate her mother’s sensitive skin. She found it almost impossible to find the right materials, so she decided to make her own. Research led her to bamboo fibers and she realised she could make a positive impact on the environment, while developing sustainable and ethical fashion.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Solenn (Nantes)
programme.desc: A former school teacher reinvents herself when she discovers a passion for sharing other women’s stories. Always inspired by the journeys of strong women, Solenn began a blog to celebrate the women in her community. Solenn’s energy and passion fueled a dream of starting her own magazine. Thanks to a successful crowdfunding campaign, this dream blossomed into a professional career in journalism and she launched a print mook (magazine & book) called Etonnantes. Etonnantes features interviews of women of all backgrounds, celebrating their diversity and success in each field.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Sophie (Nantes)
programme.desc: Always an artist at heart, Sophie grew up in the countryside amongst nature and farm animals. Her love for creating art while respecting nature turned into a drive to seek out sustainable vegetable dyes.Today, her artwork and craft are intertwined as she makes beautiful prints and motifs that she colours with natural dyes, prepared from handpicked variety of flowers and plants that turn into stunning creations.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The Bordeaux country
programme.desc: Julie meets us in Bordeaux after having surveyed the Blaye region, visited the Basin twice and travelled through the Libourne region twice, it was necessary to stop in the capital of Bordeaux one day! What better time than September to discover the pearl of Aquitaine, more often called the "City of Wine"? Julie invites the inhabitants to discover the traditions but also the more recent projects of this city full of history and gastronomy.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Poultry on the menu
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Poultry on the menu
programme.desc: Julie heads for Burgundy to discover ancient breeds of poultry reintroduced to France thanks to the work of breeder Fred Ménager. We'll then move on to Auvergne to discover how the famous Label Rouge is awarded and the role of certifying bodies ... because it's important to know what ends up on our plates. Julie also travels to the Drôme to cook guinea fowl, before heading to the Périgord to prepare a turkey stew. Julie will then head back up towards the center to discover an amazing fly maker who uses rooster feathers!

programme.title: Origami - Season 1 - The Big eagle
programme.desc: Origami is about the art of paper folding, a Japanese and Chinese millenary tradition. In modern usage, the word “origami” is used as an inclusive term for all paper folding, regardless of their cultural origin. Different folding and sculpting techniques, new models, bright colors – you’ll see all of this in our program! We will teach you how to transform a flat square sheet of paper into a finished sculpture – from simple to complex!

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 1 - The Earth does not belong to Man, Man belongs to the Earth - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 The Earth does not belong to Man, Man belongs to the Earth - Part 1
programme.desc: We end up at Yellowstone National Park in the United States, it is located in the first natural park created in the world. In Peru, the Amazon rainforest is suffering from massive deforestation. The desert on Beijing's doorstep is regularly hit by sandstorms. Some experts predict that the Chinese capital could be threatened by deserts within a hundred years. In Montana, American agricultural productivity has an ecological and human cost. Engineer Marc Dufumier explains how mechanisation is undermining the natural balance. In Brazil, agrofuels have made it possible to reduce dependence on oil, cut urban air pollution and even limit greenhouse gas emissions. In Los Angeles, human activities...

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Chocolate Tan
programme.desc: Never thought yoga and chocolate could be a healthy combination? You were wrong! Since everything is organic in California, chocolate tanning is the best way to stay glamorous while taking care of your skin with natural and delicious products. After testing this chocolate tan, we'll be sweating it out at Tantris, the three-story yoga studio in the heart of Hollywood.

A new turn, a new meeting and Paul will finally resume his dreams of painting. Follow Paul in his tumults and his happiness found.

programme.desc: The Cape Epic 2025 is one of the world's most important mountain bike stage races in the rugged landscape of South Africa. The event promises intense competition and unforgettable adventures

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2024
programme.sub-title: S01 : E16 WOF 2024 #16: Absa Cape Epic 2024 - Best of MEN
programme.desc: The ABSA Cape Epic is the world’s premier mountain bike stage race. The route changes every year, leading aspiring amateur and professional mountain bikers from around the world through 648 Untamed kilometres of unspoilt scenery and up 15 475m of climbing.

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2024
programme.sub-title: S01 : E17 WOF 2024 #17: Swatch Nines 2023 - Pedal To The Metal
programme.desc: Experience 24 minutes of adrenaline-fueled feats as Nicholi Rogatkin (USA) and Casey Brown (CAN) take viewers through the final highlight edit of the 2023 Swatch Nines Mountain Bike event at Germany's Green Hill Bikepark, held from the 14th to the 19th of August.

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2024
programme.sub-title: S01 : E21 WOF 2024 #21: Škoda Titan Desert Series - Morocco
programme.desc: Welcome to World of Freesport and the mystic deserts of Morocco, where Athletes are not only fighting nature, but also one of the hardest racetracks on the planet. The dunes… The navigation…. The suffering… The legend…. The desert! The Skoda Titan Desert Morocco.

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2023
programme.sub-title: S01 : E31 WOF 2023 #31: European Freestyle Pro Tour 2023 - Mid Season Recap
programme.desc: Die ersten Events der European Freestyle Pro Tour 2023 sind vorbei und wir werfen einen Blick zurück auf die besten Windsurfer der Welt und ihre epischen Tricks auf dem Wasser.

programme.title: GKA Kite-Surf World Cup 2024 – Sal (CPV) – Overall Highlights
programme.desc: The Qatar Airways GKA Kite World Tour is looking forward to returning to the Cape Verdean island of Sal to open the 2024 season with the discipline of kite surfing. In February, the best kite-surfing athletes will compete against each other at the Kite-Surf World Cup

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2023
programme.sub-title: S01 : E41 WOF 2023 #41: Vanora ENGADINWIND by Dakine 2023
programme.desc: Der VANORA ENGADINWIND by Dakine kehrt 2023 mit viel spannender Windsurf-Action auf dem Silvaplanasee in der Schweiz zurück. Der ENGADINWIND ist auch Gastgeber der ersten iQ FOiL U21 Weltmeisterschaft, die für das olympische Programm in Paris 2024 ausgewählt wurde.

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2018
programme.sub-title: S01 : E08 Womens Climbing Part 1 - Female Climbing Heroes - Indoor Bouldering
programme.desc: Year after year, new climbing gyms are built on all continents. The discipline of bouldering, which is unroped climbing above padded mats, primarily enjoys an increase in popularity, most notably among girls and young women.

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2018
programme.sub-title: S01 : E09 Womens Climbing Part 2 - Female Climbing Heroes - Outdoor Bouldering
programme.desc: The Forêt de Fontainebleau in France is the largest and most developed bouldering area in the world and home to one of France's most famous climbers - Mélissa Le Nevé, who was the first woman to climb the technical difficulty level 8B+

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2018
programme.desc: The 2018 Cape Epic takes riders through 658km through South Africa's Western Cape countryside and up many hills: the total elevation gain will be 13,530m. The race attracts professional and serious amateur participants from around the world.

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2018
programme.sub-title: S01 : E31 Trans Angeles - MTB - Los Angeles (USA)
programme.desc: It's time for Hans Rey to explore Southern California. What better way to do this than with Tim Commerford, a true rock star and one of mountain biking's brightest legends, Missy Giove?

programme.title: World Downhill Skateboarding Championship - Season Review - Women's Skate & Luge + Open Street Luge: 27min-HL
programme.desc: The 2025 World Downhill Skateboarding Championships season review recaps the global series held across locations including USA, UK, and Turkey. The season blends high-adrenaline competition with grassroots engagement, promoting downhill skateboarding worldwide

programme.title: World Downhill Skateboarding Championship - Season Review - Open Skate: 27min-HL
programme.desc: The 2025 World Downhill Skateboarding Championships season review recaps the thrilling global series held across locations including Utah (USA), Isle of Man (UK), and Erzincan (Turkey). The season blends high-adrenaline competition with grassroots engagement.

programme.title: Cape Epic 2026 - Live delayed Part 1
programme.desc: The 2026 Absa Cape Epic, pinnacle of the Epic Series, tears across 700 kilometres of raw South African wilderness, with 16 000 meters of climbing that will humble the unprepared and honour the relentless. Teams of two have to compete and cross the finish line together.

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2026 - WOF 2026 _25_ JACKALOPE 2026 - Virginia Beach _USA

programme.desc: 45th Gold Coast Marathon in Queensland, Australia: The world class marathon with more than 26.000 participants took place on the 6th of July 2025. It attracts annually some of the world´s most recognised marathoners on its famous flat and fast course.
